newsSinceJun6: May 2026 monthly revenue NT$9.412B, +2% MoM and +9.35% YoY (first YoY growth month; cumulative Jan-May still -6.8% YoY), driven by notebook large-size DDIC pull-ins and tablet LCD TDDI shipments, with mobile OLED flat and automotive slightly up. A consumer/display-cycle recovery signal, not AI-bottleneck evidence. No new AI design wins, M&A, capacity or regulatory news since June 6.

sources.label: UDN: Novatek May revenue NT$9.412B, YoY and MoM growth, Q2 product lines warming
TradingView/Quartr: Novatek Q4 met guidance; 2026 growth focus on AI and OLED products

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's removal case stands: Novatek's recovery (May revenue +9.35% YoY) is notebook/tablet display-cycle driven, not HBM/advanced-packaging/AI-networking exposure, so it has no claim to a top-55 node on an AI-bottleneck map. +31% YTD on a display-cycle name keeps eroding residual alpha; continue moving it down and out.
verdicts.bull: May revenue inflected to YoY growth and management's 2026 roadmap emphasizes AI and OLED products, so an edge-AI/automotive display SoC angle could re-emerge cheaply at ~13x-type fabless multiples.
verdicts.neutral: A fundamentally fine display-IC franchise in early cyclical recovery, but its AI linkage is indirect; on this page it is misclassified relative to true bottleneck nodes and should keep drifting toward removal.
verdicts.bear: Cumulative 5M revenue is still -6.8% YoY, the recovery rests on panel pull-ins that can reverse, and a 31% YTD rerating leaves the stock paying for a cycle, not an AI thesis.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete AI-edge or automotive OLED design wins, or sustained monthly revenue YoY acceleration through Q3 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Consumer/display demand pull-in reversal in H2 plus continued de-rating as the list purges low-relevance AI names.

verdicts.rankRationale: Thesis 'Low CPO exposure' is confirmed by the May print: the recovery is notebook DDIC and tablet TDDI, with zero co-packaged-optics relevance; consistent with yesterday's remove call, keep pushing it off the CPO alpha list from rank 113.
verdicts.bull: If the display cycle keeps turning, the stock can work on its own merits (margin guidance 36-39% GM, OLED DDIC mix shift) independent of CPO.
verdicts.neutral: Nothing since June 6 changes its near-zero CPO linkage; at rank 113 it carries no actionable CPO alpha either way and is dead weight on the page.
verdicts.bear: A consumer/display-cycle name up 31% YTD on a CPO list is pure classification noise; flow-driven Taiwan tech beta could unwind on any panel demand wobble.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any genuine optical I/O or silicon-photonics-adjacent product announcement (none evident).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Display/consumer cycle rollover; irrelevance to the CPO supply chain making the position untrackable as alpha.
